Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove private reader state, idempotent desired commands, bulk results, conflicts, and count repair; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect stale versions and apply a declared resolution policy with user-visible recovery rather than silent last-write assumptions.
